title: The Complete LLM Journey
concept: We traveled from scaling laws through pretraining, few-shot prompting, SFT tuning, RLHF alignment, prompt engineering, quantization, architectures, and production serving.

exercises.prompt: What does the complete LLM journey cover?

options.text: The full lifecycle from raw data to serving users

exercises.explain: We have explored the whole path, starting with raw data up to serving the finished model to millions of users!

cards.title: From Code to Companion
cards.body: How does a raw computer program transform into a chatty assistant that can code, write poetry, and translate languages? Let's take a quick trip through the entire life cycle of a Large Language Model (LLM)!

cards.title: The Three Pillars of LLMs
cards.body: An AI's journey has three key phases: Pretraining (learning from massive internet data), SFT (Supervised Fine-Tuning to learn how to chat and follow instructions), and RLHF (Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback to make it safe, helpful, and polite).

cards.title: The Chef's Journey
cards.body: Imagine building a world-class chef. Pretraining is sending them to culinary school to read every cookbook on Earth. SFT is teaching them to cook specific recipes from a menu. RLHF is having diners taste their dishes and give thumbs up or down. Finally, quantization is packing their massive kitchen tools into a small, portable food truck!

cards.title: Putting It All Together
cards.body: When you ask ChatGPT to 'Write a story about a space hamster,' it uses its pretrained language knowledge, its SFT training to follow the 'write a story' format, and its RLHF safety alignment to make sure the story is safe and appropriate for school!

cards.title: Myth: AI Learns in Real-Time
cards.body: Many people think AI models are constantly learning new facts while you chat with them. In reality, their core knowledge is locked in during training. When we use them in production, we use smart prompting and memory tricks to help them remember your current conversation.

cards.title: Before and After Alignment
cards.body: Let's look at how the AI changes from its raw form to a finished, optimized application you can run anywhere.

right.text: Polite, follows instructions, and compressed using quantization to run on your phone.

left.text: Massive, expensive, speaks in random internet text, and requires supercomputers to run.

exercises.prompt: Where does an AI model get its general, broad knowledge about the world?
exercises.explain: Pretraining is the first stage where the AI reads billions of web pages to learn basic grammar, facts, and patterns about our world.

exercises.prompt: Few-shot prompting allows you to teach an AI a new task just by showing it a few examples in your message.
exercises.explain: Few-shot prompting means giving the AI a few examples of what you want inside your prompt, without changing the AI's permanent brain weights.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing step that shrinks a massive AI model to run on smaller computers:
exercises.sentence: To fit a huge AI model onto a phone, developers use _____ to shrink its size.
exercises.explain: Quantization compresses the model's numbers so it runs faster and uses less memory, like compressing a giant video file so it plays on your phone.

exercises.prompt: Which of these responses shows an AI that has successfully undergone SFT (Supervised Fine-Tuning) to behave like a helpful assistant?

options.text: Query: 'how to bake cookies recipe easy fast'
'Here is a simple 3-step recipe for chocolate chip cookies: First...'
'Cookies! Cookies! I love cookies! Click here for more ads.'

exercises.explain: The second example is generated by an AI assistant because it follows a structured, helpful chat format, whereas the others are raw web text or search queries.

exercises.prompt: To build a safe, conversational AI, which order of steps is correct?
exercises.explain: Pretraining builds the base knowledge (reading the web), SFT teaches the chat format (acting as an assistant), and RLHF aligns the model to be helpful and safe (following human values).
